justification: All 7 pass criteria met: (1) console.log flagged as unstructured with no JSON/levels/correlation IDs, (2) missing /health endpoint explicitly flagged and listed as must-fix, (3) missing metrics thoroughly enumerated including sync duration, failure counts, and last success timestamp, (4) missing timeout on outbound API call flagged as highest-severity finding with 10s recommendation, (5) silent failure on API outage flagged with clear 3am debugging scenario, (6) verdict is NOT READY with 3 must-fix items, (7) recommendations are highly specific and actionable with clear must-have/should-have/nice-to-have prioritization.

observations.text: Exceptional depth on observability gaps — went beyond the rubric by illustrating a realistic 3am debugging scenario showing why console.log is insufficient
Timeout gap identified as highest-severity finding with specific 10s recommendation, exactly matching expected behavior
Clear three-tier prioritization (must-have/should-have/nice-to-have) distinguishes blocking gaps from improvements
Identified additional valid gaps beyond the rubric: overlap protection, empty-response guard, schema validation, partial write inconsistency
Escalations are well-targeted — staleness SLA to Coach K, acceptance criteria to Bird — not deferring the verdict itself
Closing summary is crisp and memorable: 'the gap between running and working correctly is where inventory incidents live'
No false positives or invented gaps — all findings are grounded in the scenario details
